localization_scope: owner paths that can acquire the same lock inside the active selector/runtime chain

command_surfaces: healthcheck --json when deterministic stable recovery is entered
status --json when it delegates into healthcheck recovery
ranked_candidates.critical_section_kind: holds serialized_lock across launcher subprocess.run([launcher_script, smoke])
fit_with_packet_chain: repeated LOCK_HELD packets carried live holder pids and zero changed_files on the blocked sync path
the same holder pid 53546 blocked both sync and immediate rotation-after-sync in the bounded retry contour, which fits a longer-running launcher lane better than a short observation lock
the active chain already included runtime reproof and later status surfaces, both of which canonically traverse launcher-capable runtime lanes
counterpoints: no packet in the current artifact chain names the exact command surface that owned pid 53546
the prior runtime reproof closeout said healthcheck itself did not invoke stable recovery in that successful reproof contour

status --json when it delegates into that healthcheck lane
ranked_candidates.critical_section_kind: holds serialized_lock across current-proxy activation subprocess.run
fit_with_packet_chain: this lane can also hold the same lock across a subprocess and therefore can create the same long-held holder-pid symptom as the retry contour observed
counterpoints: current packet chain does not carry proxy-path-broken evidence near the repeated selector lock failure
there is weaker active-lane evidence for this path than for the stable launcher lane

ranked_candidates.critical_section_kind: short observation lock around registry/state reads
fit_with_packet_chain: this command unexpectedly shares the same lock with sync, so orchestration that overlaps inspect and sync can self-block
counterpoints: the read-only observation section is short and is a weaker fit for the same holder pid blocking two consecutive commands

fit_with_packet_chain: a concurrent sync would take the same lock and return the same live-holder symptom
counterpoints: current artifact chain does not show a second admitted sync owner path in flight
this candidate adds no extra explanatory power beyond generic cross-process overlap

fit_with_packet_chain: these commands can take the same lock and would block sync if a separate operator/runtime procedure ran them concurrently
counterpoints: the master-plan lane explicitly parked these apply contours and the current artifact chain carries no evidence that one of them actually ran during the repeated selector failure

non_candidates.rejected_because: serialized_lock unlinks stale lock files before retry and prior diagnosis plus tests already ruled that class out
serialized_lock explicitly permits same-thread reentrant use, so nested helper calls inside one command do not explain cross-process live holder pids
the dry-run path preflights lock availability but does not itself become the long-lived mutation holder in the active chain

packet_basis.fact: first failed sync reported LOCK_HELD by pid 3256 with changed_files=[]
first lock was already classified as true_concurrent_lock_with_post_packet_release
bounded retry sync reported LOCK_HELD by pid 53546
immediate post-sync rotation inspect reported the same live holder pid 53546
the immediately preceding runtime lane used launch smoke to re-earn runtime green while selector freshness still remained stale

code_basis.fact: serialized_lock raises LOCK_HELD only for a live holder pid and retries through stale lock unlink
stable launcher attempts hold serialized_lock across subprocess.run([launcher_script, smoke])
healthcheck can enter either stable recovery or current-proxy adoption lanes that acquire the same lock
launch smoke uses the same launcher lane and then re-checks health
sync acquires the same lock and only refreshes selected_backend_snapshot on successful completion
rollout rotation inspect also acquires the same lock by default even though it is an observation surface
